data.epi_content:
I feel like throwing up.

I'm dizzy.

My stomach is nauseous.

My eyes are trembling.

Yet everything in front of me is red.

I can't hear anything.

Water is splashing and overflowing.

The smell of blood rises, making me queasy.

My hands refuse to move.

I can feel blood flowing down my mouth.

But I don't have the strength to close my jaw.

I failed.

All I had to do was pull one finger, but I couldn't even do something that simple, and now I'm like this.

I wanted to die without pain.

It hurts. It hurts. It really hurts a lot.

As time passes, I'll probably get cold.

But the pain will remain.

The bullet should have gone upward and turned my brain into meat paste.

Instead, it just lightly passed through the back of my neck.

With every breath, I taste iron in a way I can't even describe.

Well, I've tried to express my agony using various words, but I can sum it up in one simple word.

"...F...uck."

The sound of bloody water gurgling up and falling into the bathtub echoed through the bathroom, along with a foamy curse.

...............

The sound of gunpowder igniting and exploding was quite intense, even if small in scale.

Even though a pistol is small, it can easily blow off a human head, so having that impact shake my entire head from inside my mouth...

My head moved in the direction the bullet was aimed and slammed the back of my skull into the unnecessarily hard marble wall behind me.

Thanks to that, the back of my head hurts like hell.

The fact that I can think about this means the bullet didn't go upward but straight through, making a hole in my neck before flying out.

Like in movies, there's that high-pitched ringing sound when a flash grenade goes off, and my vision is shaking wildly and turning red.

The smell of gunpowder mixed with the scent of blood is enough to give a horrific feeling.

Nausea rises, but fortunately there's nothing in my stomach except a few cookies, so nothing comes up.

I stabbed my wrist for no reason.

I should have just grazed the skin and given up when it hurt.

Or I should have stopped after piercing the vein, before trying to cut the artery.

But it hurt too much.

You might press a blade to your wrist once or twice in an unconscious state, but when you come to your senses, you become too scared to continue.

They say choosing to deliberately end your life means acknowledging the absurdity and uselessness of pain in everyday life, but creating that useless pain yourself is just ridiculous.

No sane person would stab their own limbs or bite off their tongue.

How could anyone, any living being, overcome this helpless fear of losing life?

Those who manage to do it are called superhumans because they don't seem human at all.

I should have at least pulled the trigger properly.

But like an idiot, I put the gun in my mouth but couldn't angle it upward before pulling.

To shoot at my temple, I wanted my body to remain intact, and to shoot at my forehead, the gun barrel in front of my eyes was too frightening, so as a compromise, I became one of the 25% of people who attempt suicide by gun.

I shot with the gun in my mouth, but instead of dying, I'm stuttering and living like a pathetic fool.

Humans are such stupid animals.

One in four people make this stupid, idiotic mistake.

Ah, maybe there are no such statistics in this world.

Anyway, it's just devastating to have become one of them.

The result is this half-baked scene.
data.epi_content2:
Blood flows inadequately from my wrist, and there's just a hole in the back of my throat.

And humans are weaker than you'd think, but also tougher than expected, so you don't die just from having a hole in your body.

You just feel like you're dying from the pain.

Humans are so unpredictable.

Some die from minor wounds while others survive with limbs blown off and huge holes in their bodies.

I thought I belonged to the former category with this weak body, but seeing how I'm still conscious despite losing so much blood, I must be the latter.

No one, or at least I didn't think that a choice made in a moment of emotion and a longing for emptiness would deteriorate this badly.

I feel wronged.

I was living just fine.

Despite everything, I was living normally, staying locked in my room, not meeting the few acquaintances I had.

Is this punishment?

By whom, for what reason?

I just can't accept it.

"Uheugheuk..uhiiik.."

So I'm crying.

Because it hurts, because I'm sad, because I'm angry.

Because of the feeling of helplessness sinking in.

Actually, rather than crying, it feels like I'm squeezing out emotions that won't come from inside and wailing.

Usually, a girl my age would cry with slight breathing sounds, a sad atmosphere, and tears, not with this resentful sound of raising the corners of my mouth to announce my pain.

"Ueugheuk...hu, huhe."

After crying, I laugh with a deflating sound.

The sound was like air escaping from a wet balloon filled with air.

It's just so damn funny.

I may be dying, but I'm not dead yet. Well, that's just a manner of speaking.

After putting on all these airs, saying I didn't want to die ugly, not hanging myself, but in the end, I couldn't even properly blow my head off.

I'm such an idiot.

I don't have the strength to get up from this unsightly position in the bathtub.

When I try to raise my waist slightly to get out of the water, my wet, heavy clothes pull me back down.

I feel resentful.

Toward myself, or whoever dropped me here, or this useless and miserable young lady, or perhaps Evan, or Vivian, or Lydia too.

I wonder if I should blow my head off again...

But after firing one shot, the pistol fell from my hand and dropped into the water, on the left side of the bathtub.

My trembling, powerless left hand can't pick up the gun that fell into the water.

And I'm not even sure if pulling the trigger of a gun that's fallen into water would work.

If I try to twist my body slightly to pick it up with my right hand, the wound on my body stings so much that I give up.

Why didn't I think this would happen?

I should have just put it to my temple and blown my head off.

If I had, my intact head would have exploded, and brain matter would have stuck to the wall, but at least I would have died.

At least in one go, not like this.

Well, not many people can imagine it would be this ugly.

Even pessimistic people tend to view themselves optimistically.

Now that I've learned, if I fail this time, next time I'll definitely put it to my forehead or temple.

As the cliché saying goes, failure is the mother of success—at least I know now.

Still, warm water continues to pour, so as time passes and my body gets a little colder, won't I fall asleep?

Even though it hurts like hell.
data.epi_content3:
It hurts, just, well, there's nothing else to explain.

Whether it's my heart or body, it just hurts.

A coward who can't do anything right, who can't even die properly.

Someone who can't endure life and can't even properly confess that their life has no value.

I felt disgusted, ugly, and sorry for myself.

I just can't use complicated words.

I don't feel like dressing myself up with fancy words to hide.

No, before that, I can't even think of them.

With every breath, there's a wheezing sound from the hole in the back of my neck.

When I try to think of something, I just feel a sinking sensation.

I might have damaged something strange.

Well, the brain is complicated.

Still, maybe I'll look decent.

Calmly dressed, with white hair soaked in red water, a lifeless expression—maybe the corpse won't look as ugly as expected.

No matter how it happened, I'll be dead, so who would know?

"Hack...hack...."

When I inhale, the feeling of air escaping through the back with a stinging sensation is agonizing.

Ah, maybe I should have just handed the pistol to Lydia and asked her to blow my head off.

She hates me terribly, after all, having trampled my dignity like that.

If asked, she might have pulled the trigger with a bright smile.

Yes, she might have killed me in my place.

Suddenly thinking about that makes me laugh hollowly.

Though I couldn't afford the luxury of laughing out loud.

My vision blurs.

I see shadows.

They're swaying back and forth, what could they be?

Two? Three?

I don't know.

Making a blood-gurgling sound, I tried to move my mouth as much as possible.

Was what came out "save me" or "kill me"?

Even I don't know.

.

.

.

.

.

An unfamiliar ceiling.

A mirror visible when I turn my head.

An intact body. Face.

And, still snow-white hair and dull eyes that seem already dead.

An unnecessarily fancy school uniform design.

Lifeless lips.

Dark circles.

And upturned corners of the mouth.

"Haha."
